credentialFormat.description: The technical format in which credentials are encoded. Different formats support different features like selective disclosure, privacy protection, or compatibility with specific standards.

sd_jwt_vc.description: IETF JWT-based format allowing holders to selectively reveal only necessary claims using cryptographic hashing.

mdoc.description: ISO (18013-5) binary (CBOR) format for mobile documents with strong authentication and selective disclosure.

vcdm_1_1.description: W3C Verifiable Credential Data Model 1.1 using JSON Web Tokens for signing and verification.

vcdm_2_0.description: W3C Verifiable Credential Data Model 2.0 with JSON-LD support for rich semantic data and linked data capabilities.

acdc.description: Authentic Chained Data Container: a verifiable credential format based on KERI (Key Event Receipt Infrastructure) for self-certifying identifiers.

other.description: A credential format not covered by the standard formats listed above.

subjectType.description: The type of entity the credential is issued about. Indicates whether the credential describes a person, an organization, a product, or another type of entity.

Person.description: The credential is issued about a natural person (individual human being).

Organization.description: The credential is issued about a legal entity such as a company, association, or government body.

Product.description: The credential is issued about a physical or digital product.

Dataset.description: The credential is issued about a dataset or data collection.

Software.description: The credential is issued about a software application or system.

Document.description: The credential is issued about a document or formal record.

authority.description: The organization or body responsible for defining and governing the credential type. The authority determines the schema, rules, and trust framework that applies to the credential.

schemaType.description: The format used to formally define the structure and constraints of the credential's claims, enabling machine-readable validation.

JSON Schema.description: A vocabulary for annotating and validating JSON documents. Widely used to define the structure, types, and constraints of credential claims.

OCA.description: Overlays Capture Architecture: a standardized method for defining the structure, semantics, and presentation of data. Enables multi-language support and machine-readable credential schemas.
